Title: FLOURY ENDOSPERM16 encoding a NAD-dependent cytosolic malate dehydrogenase plays an important role in starch synthesis and seed development in rice

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native FLO16 promoter; Glutelin C promoter (endosperm-specific); maize Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive); CaMV 35S/double 35S for localization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes floury opaque and slightly shrunken endosperm, reduced 1000-grain weight, shorter plants, fewer tillers, reduced ATP/NADPH, elevated sucrose/protein/lipid, and impaired growth; endosperm-specific overexpression increased grain weight with little agronomic penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Central carbon metabolism linked to starch biosynthesis; malate-oxaloacetate redox shuttle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly oxidizes malate to oxaloacetate while reducing NAD+ to NADH in the cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation and proper compound starch-grain formation in rice endosperm; overexpression increases grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as a classical starch-pathway rate-limiting enzyme; functions as an important upstream metabolic/redox control point affecting activities of starch-synthesis enzymes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies reducing power/energy and upstream metabolic support for starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; mutant analysis; complementation with native gene; endosperm-specific complementation; subcellular localization; enzyme activity/zymogram assay; metabolite profiling; overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; relatively high in leaf sheath, panicle, anther, early developing grains and stem nodes; in endosperm increases from 6-15 DAF then declines at 18 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate and energy reserve determining grain filling and seed development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLO16 was cloned as Os10g0478200 encoding a NAD-dependent cytosolic malate dehydrogenase. 2. Loss of FLO16 reduced NAD-MDH activity, increased malate, decreased NADPH and ATP, and impaired starch accumulation and compound starch-grain formation in endosperm. 3. FLO16 mutation lowered expression/activity of multiple starch-biosynthetic enzymes and reduced grain weight. 4. Endosperm-specific or native complementation rescued the mutant phenotype. 5. Endosperm-specific overexpression increased grain size and 1000-grain weight, supporting breeding value.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing endosperm during 6-15 DAF; also expressed in vegetative tissues, panicles, anthers, nodes, and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream support step outside the core polymerization pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for increasing grain weight and yield by endosperm-specific overexpression or activation; can be stacked with starch-biosynthetic genes, with caution that constitutive loss causes growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study concludes CMDH mainly supports starch biosynthesis through redox homeostasis and energy supply rather than direct starch polymer formation. Exogenous malate induced AGPase activity and starch-gene expression in wild type but not in flo16.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 6-18 DAF; exogenous malate treatment; exogenous sucrose treatment
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic malate-oxaloacetate interconversion feeding endosperm starch synthesis
